mentions.text:
@matthew_sigel Fully agree with the thesis and $NODE purchase. $ETOR valuation looks like a joke at these levels.

I ended up buying few hundred thousand worth of myself after doing some slight napkin math calculations.

$2.6B valuation doesn’t make sense when ~$1.2B of it is cash.

Then they’re bringing in est. $350-$370m FCF in 2026, from some calc (fact 1% interest rate cut)

So buying a company at EV/FCF is 3.9x which looks like free real estate.

reasons: great way to get exposure without worrying about portfolio rebalancing and stock picking
